vulnerabilities.description: The upload handler accepts arbitrary extensions including .php and stores files under a web-accessible directory. Uploading a PNG named poc2.php was reported as successful and linked at /upload/poc2.php; a subsequent HEAD request to /upload/poc2.php returned 200 OK. If PHP code were uploaded, this would enable remote code execution.

The uploaded filename is embedded into the HTML without proper encoding within an href attribute. Using a filename like x' onmouseover='alert(1)'.png produced: here, enabling attribute injection and JavaScript execution in the client.
